title: system tests: add precision timestamps
body:
In run_podman(), display a nanosecond-level timestamp next to
each command and its output.

Because this clutters the results, teach logformatter to grok
these new timestamps, strip them, and display a more human-readable
time delta in the left-hand timestamp column. logformatter started off
as a mess and is now, well, 🤮. I'm sorry. I just hope its results
make it worthwhile.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

Marking WIP, because I should've injected a fault into a systest to confirm that all is working. If CI flakes, I'll use that; otherwise I'll re-push with sample failures.

Tests failed, my fault (oversight). [Sample log](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5657103231090688/html/sys-podman-fedora-37-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00189). Not the best example, because we don't see results from the in-pipeline `run_podman`... but it does show timings in a visually distinct way. I think it's a good start. 

Pushed fix, next CI run should pass. Removing WIP.
